title: Delicate Crispy Japanese Carrot Tamagoyaki
description: A crispy and salty Japanese dish from the Tohoku region, featuring carrots in a tamagoyaki form, cooked in a kadhai vessel with char-grill-yang flavor, and suitable for ayurvedic-sattvic friendly diets.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder.
Add the grated carrots to the bowl and mix until they are evenly coated with the sauce.
Heat a kadhai vessel over medium heat and add a small amount of water to the bottom.
Using a spatula, shape the carrot mixture into a tamagoyaki form and place it in the kadhai vessel.
Cook the tamagoyaki for 5 minutes on each side, or until it is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the tamagoyaki from the kadhai vessel and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess moisture.
Repeat the cooking process with the remaining carrot mixture.
Serve the crispy carrot tamagoyaki hot and enjoy.
